description_long: This object consists of a set of six swords or large knives, each with a straight, double-edged blade and a pointed tip. The blades vary in length but share a similar metallic sheen, suggesting they are forged from steel or a similar metal. The handles are dark, likely wrapped in leather or a synthetic material, and feature a subtle greenish accent at the pommel, with some having a small metallic guard. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, focusing on function over decoration. The blades are robust and slightly thick, indicating they are designed for durability and strength. The set appears to be well-crafted, with clean lines and a consistent finish across all pieces.
description: A set of six straight, double-edged swords or large knives with metallic blades and dark, wrapped handles, featuring minimal ornamentation and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length and profile of all six blades, highlighting their varying lengths and straight, pointed geometry.
description_view_1: Angled view displaying the width and thickness of the blades, as well as the handle details and subtle greenish pommel accents.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the uniformity of the handles and the clean, straight edges of the blades.
description_view_3: Angled view from the blade tips, showcasing the pointed ends and the overall symmetry of the set.
attribute_handle_design: straight, wrapped, dark with greenish pommel accent, minimal contouring
